samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. ADD SUB MULT LS RS LT LTE EQ Valid output signal for the output data. Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.

On each rising edge of the clock, if the reset signal is active, the module resets the counter and valid output signal. If the input data is valid, the module increments the counter and stores the input data in the parallel output. If the counter reaches 7, the module sets the valid output signal to 1, indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0, indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.

Implement the counter using a 3-bit register. The counter is used to count the number of serial input data bits received. The counter is incremented by 1 on each rising edge of the clock when the input data is valid. The counter is reset to 0 when the reset signal is active.

Implement the valid output signal as a register. The valid output signal is set to 1 when the counter reaches 7 and the input data is valid. The valid output signal is set to 0 when the reset signal is active.

Implement the parallel output as a register. The parallel output is set to the input data on each rising edge of the clock when the input data is valid.

Implement the following logic in the always block triggered on the rising edge of the clock. If the reset signal is active, cnt=0 and dout_valid_reg=0. If the input data is valid, dout_parallel[7-cnt]<=din_valid. If cnt==7, dout_valid_reg=1, cnt=0. Else, cnt=cnt+1.
